question_text: What is a moment in physics?

options.label: A
options.text: The turning effect of a force about a pivot or fulcrum

options.label: B
options.text: The speed at which an object is moving

options.label: C
options.text: The amount of matter in an object

options.label: D
options.text: The force pushing an object forward

explanation: A moment is defined as the turning effect produced by a force acting at a distance from a pivot point. This is a key concept in understanding rotational forces and how levers work.
